source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/07_Retrofit (HnX-R1)/★ FDA 510(k) - K252912/참고자료/참고 - Cybersecurity - 최종 정리/3.1_A-CAV-HNX_Architecture View.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity architecture view for the HnX-R1 digital X-ray imaging system, covering FDA architectural views, data flow, updateability, and security use cases.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Shows a cyber security architecture view for Digital X-ray Imaging System model HnX-R1 and maps risks to STRIDE, cybersecurity controls, and IEC 60601-4-5 requirements.
keyPoints: Scope is limited to HnX-R1, the Digital X-ray Imaging System.
The FDA architectural views addressed are Global system view, Updateability/Patchability view, and Security use case view; Multi patient harm view is excluded.
The detector update process uses the standard communication procedure, sends a *.bpd update file, decrypts patch data, performs a SHA-256 integrity check, and reboots after applying the update.
Risk identification is provided for CRM-01 through CRM-32 according to the Data-Flow Diagram and CRM-33 through CRM-67 according to IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirements.
The document refers to penetration test report no. EBB-2025-000109 for PT-1 through PT7 evaluation results.

evidence.claim: The document is a cybersecurity architecture view for the HnX-R1 digital X-ray imaging system.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of this document is to show a cyber security architecture view for Digital X-ray Imaging System (Model name: HnX-R1).

evidence.claim: The scope of the document is limited to HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is limited to HnX-R1, the Digital X-ray Imaging System.

evidence.claim: The document addresses three FDA architectural views and excludes Multi patient harm view.
evidence.evidenceText: three of the four architectural views corresponding to the FDA requirements are Global system view, Updateability/Patchability view, and Security use case view. Multi patient harm view is excluded because it is not our product.

evidence.claim: The detector update process uses only the standard communication procedure and no additional ports.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector's update process can only proceed after a proper connection has been established through the standard communication procedure. No additional ports are used.

evidence.claim: The console sends a *.bpd update file to the detector and initiates the patch request.
evidence.evidenceText: The console sends the update file (*.bpd) to the detector and initiates the patch request.

evidence.claim: The detector decrypts patch data and performs a SHA-256 integrity check during update.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector decrypts the received patch data and performs a SHA-256 integrity check.

evidence.claim: After a normal update response, the detector applies the update and automatically reboots.
evidence.evidenceText: After sending a normal response, the detector proceeds with applying the update and automatically reboots itself.

evidence.claim: Data-flow risk identification uses STRIDE threat categories.
evidence.evidenceText:

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-4-5 cybersecurity requirements are used for risk identification.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk identification according to cybersecurity requirements (IEC 60601-4-5)

evidence.claim: The document maps risk IDs to cybersecurity control categories including authentication, authorization, cryptography, integrity, confidentiality, logging, recovery, and update controls.
evidence.evidenceText:
A) Authentication controls: Risk ID CRM-33-40

B) Authorization controls: Risk ID CRM-63-67

C) Cryptography controls: Risk ID CRM-61

D) Code, data, and execution integrity controls: Risk ID: CRM-48-54, 58

E) Confidentiality controls: Risk ID CRM-59-60

F) Event detection and logging controls: Risk ID CRM-41-47

G) Resiliency and recovery controls: Risk ID CRM-62

H) Firmware and software update controls: Risk ID CRM-55,56, 57

evidence.claim: Penetration test evaluation results are referenced in report no. EBB-2025-000109.

reviewFlags.reason: Document ref no. field is present but blank in the source text.
Authority is supported by text referring to FDA requirements, but the document does not include an FDA header or direct FDA-issued identifier.
Some OCR or source text artifacts appear in risk references, including inconsistent CRM/CMR text and misspellings such as 'Attemps'.
